info.title: haiflow API
info.description:
HTTP orchestrator for Claude Code. Start Claude Code sessions in tmux, trigger prompts, queue work, stream and capture responses, fan out across worker pools, and chain agents with pub/sub.

Every endpoint except `GET /health`, `GET /version`, the `POST /hooks/*` callbacks, and `POST /ingest/{source}` requires an `Authorization: Bearer <HAIFLOW_API_KEY>` header. `HAIFLOW_API_KEY` is a secret you define, not an Anthropic/Claude paid key.

servers.description: Behind a Cloudflare Tunnel / reverse proxy (see Deployment)

tags.name: Sessions
tags.description: Start, stop, list, inspect, and steer Claude Code tmux sessions.

tags.name: Triggers
tags.description: Send prompts to a session, sent immediately when idle, auto-queued when busy.

tags.name: Responses
tags.description: Fetch, stream, and clear task responses and tool/diff timelines.

tags.name: Queue
tags.description: Inspect and manage a session's pending prompt queue.

tags.name: Tasks
tags.description: The durable task ledger: what each task ran, changed, spent, and how long it took.

tags.name: Usage & Savings
tags.description: Measured token consumption and the equivalent API cost avoided.

tags.name: Pools & Map-Reduce
tags.description: Load-balance and fan work across a pool of member sessions, then reduce.

tags.name: Pipeline
tags.description: Event-driven agent chains over pub/sub topics.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Signed inbound webhook gateway: drive tasks from a SaaS webhook without sharing your bearer key.

post.description: Start a Claude Code session in a detached tmux session. Blocks until the SessionStart hook links a Claude session id (so responses can be captured), or fails with `409` once `HAIFLOW_START_READY_TIMEOUT_MS` elapses, almost always because the Claude Code hooks are not wired (`haiflow setup`).

cwd.description: Working directory for Claude. **Optional.** Precedence: if `HAIFLOW_CWD` is pinned it is forced (a different requested `cwd` is overridden, not rejected, and the response carries `cwdOverridden: true`). Otherwise, when `HAIFLOW_ALLOW_REQUEST_CWD=false`, every start is rejected with `400` (pin `HAIFLOW_CWD` instead). When neither applies and `cwd` is omitted, haiflow falls back to `/tmp` and the response includes `cwdDefaulted: true`.

ready.description: `false` when the session linked but TUI readiness could not be confirmed in time (still usable).

cwdOverridden.description: Present and `true` when the server has a forced `HAIFLOW_CWD` differing from the requested `cwd`.

cwdDefaulted.description: Present and `true` when `cwd` was omitted (and no `HAIFLOW_CWD` is pinned) so the server fell back to `/tmp`.

400.description: `HAIFLOW_ALLOW_REQUEST_CWD=false` with no `HAIFLOW_CWD` pinned: every `/session/start` is rejected (set `HAIFLOW_CWD` on the server instead).

409.description: The session never linked a Claude session id (hooks likely not wired). The dead pane is torn down.

post.description: Remove an offline session's state, queue, and responses. Only works on offline sessions.
Bulk-remove offline sessions whose last status change (`since`) is older than a TTL (default `HAIFLOW_SESSION_TTL_HOURS`, 24h). Idle/busy and recently-offline sessions are kept.

olderThanHours.description: Override the TTL for this call.

post.summary: Interrupt or steer a running session
post.description: Send a control key into a running session's TUI, optionally followed by a steering prompt. Use it to unstick a session wedged on a permission prompt, or to redirect a running agent.

mode.description: `escape` cancels the current generation/tool without exiting; `ctrl-c` is harsher.

prompt.description: A steering prompt typed in after the interrupt settles. Passes the same structural checks as `/trigger`.
prompt.example: stop and run the tests instead

400.description: Invalid body, or the steering `prompt` was rejected by structural checks.

500.description: Failed to send the interrupt to the session.

waiting.description: Present and `true` when the session is blocked needing input; `status` stays `busy`.

post.description: Send a prompt to Claude. If Claude is busy, the prompt is auto-queued and sent when idle. Supports scheduling, dedup, priority, and fire-and-forget (`ephemeral` + `callbackUrl`).

prompt.description: The prompt or slash command to send.

source.description: Label for where the trigger came from.

priority.description: Higher drains first; ties break FIFO.

dedupKey.description: A second enqueue with the same key (while one is running or queued) is dropped.

delaySeconds.description: Schedule the task for later. The drain picks it up once due, even on an idle session.

notBefore.description: ISO timestamp form of `delaySeconds` (takes precedence if both given).

ephemeral.description: Fire-and-forget: auto-start an offline session (needs `cwd`), then stop it once this task responds (only if nothing else is queued).

cwd.description: Working directory used only when `ephemeral` auto-starts an offline session.

callbackUrl.description: POST the result here on completion. Off unless `HAIFLOW_ALLOW_TRIGGER_CALLBACK=true`.

notBefore.description: Present when the task was scheduled for later.

400.description: Invalid request (e.g. `ephemeral` with no resolvable `cwd`, or a `callbackUrl` that is disabled or off the allowlist).

500.description: Failed to send the prompt to the tmux session.

503.description: Session is offline (and `ephemeral` did not auto-start it).

get.description:
Stream response status via Server-Sent Events. Opens a persistent connection that emits `status`, then a terminal `complete` / `error` / `timeout` event. Pass the **same** `session` you triggered on.

Event stream (text/event-stream):
- `status`: `{"id":"...","status":"pending"}` or `{"status":"queued","position":2}`
- `complete`: the full response object (same shape as `GET /responses/{id}`)
- `error`: `{"error":"Session is offline"}`
- `timeout`: emitted when the timeout is reached

parameters.name: session
parameters.description: Session that owns the task. Omitting it silently streams the `default` session.

200.description: The tool/command/diff timeline for the task.

get.description: List tasks from the durable SQLite ledger, newest first. Each task records the ordered tool calls, commands run, files changed, real diffs, token usage, model, timing, and estimated savings.

200.description: Task detail, including the saved response `messages` when available.

post.description: Cancel a single task without killing the warm session or clearing the whole queue. If it is the running task, sends Escape and drains the next queued prompt; if it is queued, removes just that item.

404.description: The id is neither running nor queued for that session.

get.description: Aggregate token usage and the equivalent API cost avoided since a timestamp (default: last 24h).

Rolling 5-hour and 7-day windows (the subscription rate-limit windows), for the whole account or one `session`. When `HAIFLOW_USAGE_ALERT_TOKENS` is set, `alert` is `true` once the 5h window crosses it (alert-only, never throttles).

post.summary: Load-balance a prompt across a pool
post.description: Send one prompt to a pool defined in `pipeline.json`. It goes to an idle member, or the least-loaded member's queue.

priority.description: Queue priority if the prompt is queued behind a member's work.

400.description: `prompt` is required or was rejected by structural checks.

post.summary: Map items across a pool, then reduce
post.description: Fan a list of items across a pool in parallel, then fire a reducer once every item has come back (the fan-in / JOIN). If a shard never returns, the reducer fires with partial results after `HAIFLOW_MAP_TIMEOUT_SEC` (default 1800).

items.description: Items to map over (strings, or objects stringified into `{{item}}`). Capped at `HAIFLOW_MAP_MAX_ITEMS` (200).

reduce.description: Optional reducer fired once all items return. The promptTemplate supports `{{results}}` (joined outputs), `{{total}}`, `{{pool}}`, and `{{runId}}`.

promptTemplate.example:
Merge these reviews into one report:
{{results}}

400.description: Invalid request: invalid JSON, empty `items`, or missing `mapTemplate`.

get.description: Get the current pipeline configuration, Redis status, and recent events.

post.description: Publish an event to a pipeline topic. Useful for external systems (n8n, scripts) to inject work into the pipeline. The source session is recorded as `external`.

message.description: Passed to subscriber prompt templates as `{{message}}`.
message.example: New design for the login page: ...

200.description: Event published. An unknown topic is a silent no-op (still 200).

400.description: Invalid body, missing `topic`/`message`, or the message was rejected by structural checks.

get.description: Recent pipeline events with their full per-subscriber delivery records. Distinct from `GET /pipeline`, which embeds only the 10 most recent events in summarized form.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max events to return (default 50, capped at 200).

post.description: Drive a Claude task (or publish an event) directly from a SaaS webhook. **Not** bearer-authenticated. Authenticity is a per-source HMAC signature over the raw body, with one-use replay protection. Configure recipes in `ingest.json`. Every payload field is wrapped in a `BEGIN/END WEBHOOK DATA` frame as untrusted input.

parameters.name: X-Haiflow-Timestamp
parameters.description: Optional timestamp for the `hmac-sha256` scheme. When present it is enforced for freshness and signed as `${ts}.${rawBody}`.

requestBody.description: The raw provider payload. The signature is verified over the raw bytes, so do not re-serialize before forwarding.

200.description: Accepted: triggered or published per the recipe.

400.description: Rejected by structural prompt checks, or a `publish` recipe is missing its `topic`.

404.description: Unknown ingest source (no matching recipe in `ingest.json`).

413.description: Raw payload or rendered prompt exceeds `MAX_PROMPT_SIZE` (512KB).

503.description: Redis unavailable and the gateway fails closed (unless `HAIFLOW_INGEST_ALLOW_WITHOUT_REDIS=true`).

get.description: Catches the #1 silent failure: a tmux session whose hooks were never wired (so the Stop hook never fires and the session sits busy forever). The tell is `tmuxRunning: true` with `hooksLinked: false`. With no `session`, returns reports for all sessions.
Unauthenticated. Returns the running build so operators can confirm which version a remote haiflow is on.

bearerAuth.description: Your `HAIFLOW_API_KEY`, compared in constant time. This is the root credential: anyone holding it can run code in your sessions.

example: Here's a summary of recent commits...

seq.description: Ordinal position of the step in the task.

detail.description: Tool detail; for Edit/Write/MultiEdit this is the mini-diff.

prompt.description: The prompt that started the task.

chain.description: Pipeline chain id when the task was driven by a pipeline event.

error.description: Error detail when the task failed or timed out.
